varieties.name: Dwarf Cavendish
varieties.traits: Compact plant 1.2-2.1m high, short stature ideal for backyards
varieties.growing_notes: Well-adapted to humid subtropical climates subtropical climate. Performs well with high-density planting and drip irrigation. Thick peel retains greenish color even when ripe. Highly susceptible to Sigatoka in humid conditions.
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries, tissue culture suppliers

varieties.name: Williams
varieties.traits: 2.4-3.5m high, dark brown/black/red streaks on pseudostem, red leaf coloration
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, fastest time from planting to harvest among Cavendish

varieties.name: Grand Naine
varieties.traits: 3-5m tall, Giant Cavendish type, robust growth

varieties.name: Robusta
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts to humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Highly susceptible to Sigatoka leaf spot in humid tropics, requires disease management. Good for table use.
varieties.availability: Queensland and NSW nurseries

varieties.name: Lady Finger (Sugar Banana)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, introduced to Australia from Pacific Islands 1800s
varieties.traits: Tall variety 6-7.5m (some dwarf forms 2-5m), slender cigar-like fruit
varieties.yield_notes: 10-14 hands with 12-20 fingers per hand, 10-20 pound bunches

varieties.name: Dwarf Lady Finger
varieties.origin: Bred from Lady Finger for compact growth
varieties.traits: Compact 2-4m height, easier to manage for harvesting
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer with heavy bunches for size
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent disease, wind and cold resistance for humid subtropical conditions. Tissue culture produced for disease-free stock. Select suckers with thicker stems to avoid reversion.
varieties.availability: Queensland tissue culture suppliers, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Pisang Ceylon (Pisang Ceylan, Mysore)
varieties.origin: India, improved virus-free variety of Mysore banana
varieties.traits: 3-5m tall, attractive pink/maroon midribs on leaves, pinkish copper young leaves

varieties.name: Blue Java (Ice Cream Banana)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, also called Hawaiian banana, Namwah
varieties.traits: Fast-maturing 3-6m tall, wide wind-resistant leaves, lime green foliage

varieties.name: Ducasse (Thai Banana, Kluai Nam Wa)
varieties.origin: Northern Thailand (Wa River Basin), introduced Australia 19th century

varieties.name: Dwarf Ducasse (Kluai Namwa Khom)
varieties.traits: Compact 2-3m height, ideal for small backyards

varieties.name: Goldfinger (FHIA-01)
varieties.origin: Honduras 1988, Honduran Foundation for Agricultural Research
varieties.traits: Dwarf 2.4-3.5m (8-12 feet), compact sturdy, strong pseudostem, high wind resistance
varieties.yield_notes: Solid producer, 100 lb bunches with no propping, hands mature sequentially
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates SUBTROPICS - performs BEST in subtropical conditions with superior flavor vs tropical zones! Outstanding cold tolerance, wind resistance, disease resistance (Fusarium wilt Race 1, Black Sigatoka resistant, Yellow Sigatoka tolerant). PERMITTED for SE QLD. Can grow without pesticides.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Red Dacca (Red Banana)
varieties.traits: 4-5m tall, attractive red hue to pseudostems
varieties.growing_notes: Higher beta carotene and vitamin C than yellow bananas. Shows promise in cooler urban humid subtropical climates. Can be eaten fresh or cooked (baked, fried, dried). Queensland tissue culture available.
varieties.availability: Queensland DPI tissue culture ($3 each), specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Pacific Plantain
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts to humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Used in Pacific Island cuisine.
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries, Blue Sky Backyard Bananas

varieties.name: Senorita
varieties.traits: Compact growth suitable for humid subtropical climates gardens
varieties.growing_notes: Now available in Queensland after regulation changes. Adapts to subtropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries, Blue Sky Backyard Bananas

varieties.name: Musa banksii 🌏
varieties.origin: NATIVE to Australia - Cape York to south of Townsville, Queensland
varieties.traits: Wild banana species, grows in rainforest
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS AUSTRALIAN wild banana. More common across range. Cannot cross with cultivated bananas. Of botanical interest only. Historical and scientific significance.
varieties.availability: Not commercially available, wild species - try Cairns Botanical Gardens or SGAP

varieties.name: Musa jackeyi 🌏
varieties.origin: NATIVE to coastal north Queensland - VERY RARE
varieties.traits: Wild species, stumpy reddish fruit on vertical stem
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS AUSTRALIAN wild banana, EXTREMELY RARE even in normal range. Normal range largely uninhabited. Of botanical interest only.
varieties.availability: Extremely rare, not commercially available

varieties.name: Nendran (Nenthiram, Ethapazham) 🌏
varieties.origin: Southern India, growing wild since ancient times, Tropical monsoon MOST ICONIC BANANA
varieties.yield_notes: 12-15kg bunches with 5-6 hands, 40-50 fruits per bunch

varieties.name: Palayankodan (Palayam Kodan) 🌏
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates, regional name for Poovan type
varieties.traits: Small variety, thickly clustered bunches, medium bunches

varieties.name: Ney Poovan 🌏
varieties.origin: Southern India, traditional in Tamil Nadu/Karnataka, now large-scale in tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Slender plant, previously backyard crop now commercial, 12-14 months to fruit
varieties.growing_notes: Most delicate diploid cultivar. Tolerant to leaf spot but susceptible to Fusarium wilt. Thrives in tropical monsoon climates tropical climate. Excellent shelf life.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates commercial and backyard growers

varieties.name: Rasthali (Rasakadali) 🌏
varieties.origin: Karnataka, medium-tall variety, HIGHLY PRIZED traditional cultivar
varieties.traits: Medium tall, yellowish green stem with brownish blotches, reddish petiole margin, 5-7 hands
varieties.growing_notes: HIGHLY PRIZED for table use. EXTREMELY SWEET, perfect for desserts and smoothies. Longer crop duration (15 months). Severe susceptibility to Fusarium wilt. Requires bunch cover for sun cracking protection. Formation of hard lumps makes production expensive. Popular in tropical monsoon climates but challenging. Available year-round.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates markets, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu suppliers

varieties.name: Karpuravalli 🌏
varieties.origin: Southern districts of Tamil Nadu and tropical monsoon climates, also Bihar as "Kanthali"
varieties.traits: Tall robust plant, well-suited to marginal lands and soils, low input conditions
varieties.yield_notes: 8-9 compact hands, 13-14 fingers per hand, 100-120 fruits per bunch
varieties.growing_notes: Highly susceptible to wilt but tolerant to leaf spot. Well-suited for DROUGHT and SALT conditions. Thrives even in alkaline soils. Popular for desserts and fresh eating. 12-14 months crop duration. Can be grown under marginal conditions PERFECT for tropical monsoon climates varied terrains.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu markets and nurseries

varieties.name: Annaan 🌏
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Mannan (Malayannan) 🌏
varieties.origin: Northern tropical monsoon climates (Wayanad and Kannur districts)
varieties.traits: Regional favorite in northern tropical monsoon climates
varieties.growing_notes: "Every district in tropical monsoon climates has its favourite banana" - this is Wayanad and Kannur's choice. Shows strong regional preference. Thrives in monsoon conditions.
varieties.availability: Wayanad and Kannur region markets

varieties.name: Peyan 🌏
varieties.origin: Mostly Tamil Nadu, favorite in Kollam district tropical monsoon climates
varieties.growing_notes: Favorite in Kollam district but not popular elsewhere in tropical monsoon climates. Demonstrates strong regional taste preferences. Adapts to southern tropical monsoon climates monsoon climate.
varieties.availability: Kollam district tropical monsoon climates, Tamil Nadu suppliers

varieties.name: Kumpillaannan 🌏
varieties.growing_notes: Part of tropical monsoon climates traditional banana diversity. Monsoon-adapted.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates traditional markets

varieties.name: Kaliyethan 🌏
varieties.growing_notes: Part of tropical monsoon climates traditional diversity. Monsoon-adapted.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates traditional markets

varieties.name: Pachakkappa 🌏
varieties.traits: Traditional tropical monsoon climates, possible green-type

varieties.name: Matti 🌏
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu and tropical monsoon climates traditional

varieties.name: Padatti 🌏
varieties.growing_notes: Part of tropical monsoon climates traditional banana diversity. Monsoon-adapted.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates local markets

varieties.name: Agnibanana
varieties.origin: Banana Research Station, Kannara, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University - Agniswar x Pisang Lilin
varieties.growing_notes: REMARKABLE early ratooning ability - completes FOUR CROPS IN THREE YEARS. Resistant to leaf spot, Fusarium wilt, and burrowing nematode. Promising hybrid for tropical monsoon climates monsoon climate with disease resistance.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, Banana Research Station Kannara

varieties.name: Anakhia
varieties.origin: Banana Research Station, Kannara, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University - Vannan x Pisang Lilin
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for tropical monsoon climates conditions with compact fruits. Good adaptation to monsoon climate.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, Banana Research Station Kannara

varieties.name: Yelakki
varieties.growing_notes: HIGHLY VALUED in India for exceptional sweetness. Small size makes it popular for snacking. Available in specialty markets both regions.
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers both humid subtropical climates and tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Malbhog
varieties.growing_notes: Known for fragrant flavor. Popular choice for special celebrations. Adapts to tropical monsoon climates monsoon.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates and Indian specialty markets

varieties.name: Chakkarakeli
varieties.growing_notes: Poovan-type cultivar popular in Andhra Pradesh. Also grown in tropical monsoon climates. Monsoon-adapted.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates and Andhra Pradesh suppliers
